findings.evidence: `nl -ba .../TensorTrivial.lean` shows only `namespace TauCeti`/`end TauCeti`; `rg -n "groupLike.*\*|tensor.*groupLike|tensor.*trivial" code/TauCeti` finds only the module docstring and `FiniteTrivial.trivialTensor`.
findings.fix: Either remove the public module/import if the existing `Comodule.groupLike`/`Comodule.trivial` API is intended to suffice, or add the named tensor-product API needed by the roadmap target with simp-characterization lemmas.
findings.issue: The public `TensorTrivial` module is imported from `TauCeti.lean` but declares no tensor-product objects, morphisms, or characteristic lemmas.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "FGComoduleCat\.trivial|trivialTensor|trivial_obj|trivialTensor_coact" code/TauCeti` finds only the declarations/docstrings; the existing finite API has the parallel `of_obj` and `of_coact` lemmas in `Comodule/Finite.lean:101` and `Comodule/Finite.lean:108`.
findings.fix: Add characteristic lemmas such as `trivial_obj`, `trivial_coact`, `trivialTensor_obj`, and `trivialTensor_coact` relating them to `ComoduleCat.trivial`, `Comodule.trivial`, and the tensor-product module.
findings.issue: `FGComoduleCat.trivial` and `FGComoduleCat.trivialTensor` are new public objects but have no `@[simp]` lemmas identifying their underlying `ComoduleCat` object or coaction.

summary: The new public tensor module does not expose any tensor-product API, and the new finitely generated trivial objects lack characteristic lemmas. Downstream code would have to rely on unfolding abbrev bodies instead of using named simp/API lemmas.
